subject: [OOM] mutualize sphinx linting
commit_message:
[OOM] mutualize sphinx linting

docs and docs-linkcheck are both using sphinx
and can run easily in parallel without interfering.
Both jobs take less than 3 minutes.
This is often less than the allocation time of a jenkins minion.
All of this make the mutualization of these 2 jobs wishable.

- use one subproject for docs and docs-linkcheck tox profile.
- enable tox parallel mode

Issue-ID: OOM-2648
Signed-off-by: guillaume.lambert <guillaume.lambert@orange.com>
Change-Id: I2f61fa489ca7ab543a667d07009bf32929329456

entries.message:
Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/ci-management-jjb-verify/5678/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/ci-management-jjb-verify/5678

Patch Set 1:

I don't see the point to split tox lists in multiple runs.
For a very small reduce of duration (not so sure), you would have quickly to manage excludes per branch.

Tox could be the only entry point and then you would only define env lists per branches.
Here it asks for backports or to add exclude per stream.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> I don't see the point to split tox lists in multiple runs.
> For a very small reduce of duration (not so sure), you would have quickly to manage excludes per branch.
> 
> Tox could be the only entry point and then you would only define env lists per branches.
> Here it asks for backports or to add exclude per stream.

I don't see the point to split tox lists too. I'll let OOM committers answer you on this point.
Whatever the rreason is, I think that at least docs and docs-linkcheck should be regrouped as proposed here.

I don't think there is a need to exclude per stream here contrary to https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/oom/+/120407
Both docs and docs-linkcheck tox profiles are present in master and frankfurt and guilin branches.
Change has been successfully merged by Morgan Richomme
